title: Model Failover Flow
interaction_flow: AiManager → AiManager: textProvider($name) → validates TextProvider interface
AiManager → AiManager: hasFakeGatewayFor($agent) → bool [swap in fake gateway if faked]
Promptable → Promptable: getDefaultModelFor(Provider $provider) [if model is null]
PrimaryProvider --x Promptable: throws FailoverableException (RateLimitedException | ProviderOverloadedException | InsufficientCreditsException)
Promptable → FallbackProvider: prompt(new AgentPrompt(...)) [retry with fallback]
ALT all providers exhausted: Promptable → Caller: throw $lastException ?? RuntimeException('No AI providers were configured.')
failover_conditions: FailoverableException is the marker interface — only these trigger failover
provider_resolution: 2. Agent method provider() / model() checked next
5. Array of providers enables multi-step failover list via formatProviderAndModelList()
model_resolution: If model is null after provider lookup: getDefaultModelFor(Provider) is called

key_methods.Promptable::prompt: public function prompt(string $prompt, array $attachments=[], Lab|array|string|null $provider=null, ?string $model=null, ?int $timeout=null): AgentResponse

key_relationships: Promptable (trait) uses AiManager via Ai facade static calls
Promptable depends_on AgentPrompt (value object built per call)
Promptable fires AgentFailedOver event on each failover
AiManager resolves concrete providers: Anthropic, OpenAi, Gemini, Azure, Cohere, etc.
PrimaryProvider / FallbackProvider both implement TextProvider contract with prompt() and stream()
important_notes: The failover list is built ONCE before the loop via formatProviderAndModelList — supports single string, Lab enum, or array of providers. Each iteration gets a fresh textProviderFor() call. The AgentFailedOver event (not ProviderFailedOver) is fired for agent-level failovers. stream() follows the same withModelFailover path. Fake gateway injection happens inside textProviderFor(), transparent to failover logic.
